content: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 Stealth AI Startup: Remote Nuanced AI Data Training Anywhere in the World 🇦🇷 Argentina, 🇨🇴 Colombia, 🇨🇷 Costa Rica, 🇮🇳 India, 🇲🇽 Mexico, 🇵🇪 Peru, and 🇵🇭 Philippines Oregon Customer Support Contract <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Seattle, WA <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/howie-howie/">https://www.linkedin.com/company/howie-howie/</a> </p> <p>We’re building AI systems that help busy professionals manage scheduling, communication, and operational coordination at a very high standard.</p> <p>We’re looking for sharp, fast-thinking operators to help train and improve our AI executive assistant systems.</p> <p>This is not traditional data labeling. The role requires judgment, attention to detail, communication intuition, and the ability to make nuanced decisions quickly in fast-moving situations.</p> <h2>What You’ll Do</h2> <ul> <li>Review and improve AI-generated scheduling and communication workflows</li> <li>Identify unclear, risky, or incomplete AI responses</li> <li>Make judgment calls around calendars, timing conflicts, customer preferences, and tone</li> <li>Ensure messages are specific, actionable, and context-aware</li> <li>Flag edge cases and operational issues that could impact customer experience</li> <li>Help train the AI to behave more like a thoughtful human assistant</li> <li>Work quickly while maintaining strong accuracy and consistency</li> </ul> <h2>Ideal Candidates</h2> <p>You may be a fit if you:</p> <ul> <li>Thrive in fast-paced environments</li> <li>Have strong written communication instincts</li> <li>Naturally catch details others miss</li> <li>Can think critically without relying heavily on rigid rules</li> <li>Are comfortable making decisions with imperfect information</li> <li>Learn systems quickly and adapt to changing workflows</li> <li>Have experience in operations, executive assistance, hospitality, customer support, coordination, recruiting, or similar high-context work</li> </ul> <p>You do <strong>not</strong> need to be an engineer or technical expert.</p> <h2>Requirements</h2> <ul> <li>Excellent English communication skills</li> <li>Strong internet connection</li> <li>MacBook required</li> <li>Ability to work independently and manage time effectively</li> <li>Comfortable receiving direct feedback and iterating quickly</li> <li>Available for consistent weekly hours</li> </ul> <h2>Nice to Have</h2> <ul> <li>Experience supporting executives or clients</li> <li>Familiarity with AI tools such as <a class="decorated-link" href="https://chatgpt.com?utm_source=chatgpt.com">ChatGPT</a> or <a class="decorated-link" href="https://claude.ai?utm_source=chatgpt.com">Claude</a></li> <li>Experience in startups, operations, scheduling, or remote support environments</li> <li>Strong pattern recognition and organizational instincts</li> </ul> <h2>What Success Looks Like</h2> <p>Successful trainers are:</p> <ul> <li>Fast without being careless</li> <li>Calm under pressure</li> <li>Thoughtful communicators</li> <li>Highly reliable</li> <li>Able to balance customer experience with operational efficiency</li> <li>Comfortable owning decisions rather than escalating every edge case</li> </ul> <p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/stealth-ai-startup-remote-nuanced-ai-data-training">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/stealth-ai-startup-remote-nuanced-ai-data-training</a></p> Thu, https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/stealth-ai-startup-remote-nuanced-ai-data-training SuperPlane: DevRel - Content Creator Anywhere in the World Delaware Technical Writing, DevOps, Content Creation, CI/CD Pipelines, and AI Agents Sales and Marketing Full-Time <img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/4898/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" /> <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> USA <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://superplane.com">https://superplane.com</a> </p> <p>SuperPlane is an open source control plane where AI agents and engineers run operations together. We're a team of 10, founded by the creators of Semaphore CI/CD. Our users are DevOps and platform engineers at mid-size to large engineering orgs.</p> <p>We have a product engineers love, an open source repo with 2K+ stars, and events in the pipeline. What we don't have is someone dedicated to showing the world what SuperPlane can do. We ship fast but our content doesn't keep up. We need someone who can turn what we build into blog posts, tutorials, demos, and videos that resonate with practitioners.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2>What you'll own</h2> <p>This is our first DevRel hire. Your priorities, in order:</p> <p><strong>1. Video content</strong>: Create screencasts, product walkthroughs, and video demos that show SuperPlane in action. You're comfortable on camera and can produce polished content without a production team.</p> <p><strong>2. Technical content</strong>: Build working use cases and turn them into blog posts, tutorials, and guides. The typical flow: explore the product, develop a real workflow, write it up, then turn it into a video.</p> <p><strong>3. Community presence</strong>: Be SuperPlane's voice in our Discord, on Reddit, Hacker News, and relevant DevOps Slack groups. Welcome new users, answer questions, surface interesting discussions, and represent us in a natural, non-salesy way.</p> <p><strong>4. Events (occasional)</strong>: Represent SuperPlane at conferences or meetups a few times per year.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2>What success looks like</h2> <p>In the first 6 months:</p> <ul> <li> <p>A consistent publishing cadence: written content and videos shipping weekly</p> </li> <li> <p>Content that drives meaningful traffic and signups from organic search and social media shares</p> </li> <li> <p>Active, visible presence in community channels where our users hang out</p> </li> <li> <p>A growing library of use-case demos that the sales and product teams can reference</p> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2>You're a fit if</h2> <ul> <li> <p><strong>You have a DevOps or platform engineering background.</strong>&nbsp;You've managed deployments, written CI pipelines, used tools like Terraform or Kubernetes. You could still be an engineer if you wanted to, but you prefer making content.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>You can write working code.</strong>&nbsp;Your tutorials include real, runnable examples. You can spin up a staging environment, write a Lambda function, or put t
